exemplar.title:
How does Pink Floyd's The Wall serve as a cautionary tale through the
portrayal of isolation?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does Pink Floyd's The Wall serve as a cautionary tale through the
portrayal of isolation?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Consistently communicates the topic with clear purpose and focus
Research question is precisely stated, appropriately narrow, and integrated throughout
Section headings and contents page effectively signpost the investigative path
weaknesses: Methodology lacks full transparency in source-selection rationale
Occasional background digressions slightly reduce concision
Minor lapses in topic sentences at key analytical junctures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic clarity and research question) clearly sit in the 5â6 band, while methodology meets the 3â4 band. On balance, the investigation demonstrates accurate and effective communication with a focused and appropriate question, but the methodologyânotably the limited range of scholarly sources and patchy citation transparencyâprevents a top-band 6. A score of 5 reflects a high-quality, purposeful approach with room for greater methodological breadth and clarity.

strengths: Coherent and accurate knowledge of the narrative arc and thematic content
Consistent and mostly precise use of literary and musical terminology
Effective interdisciplinary application of musical analysis to lyrical themes
weaknesses: Reliance on some journalistic rather than peer-reviewed sources weakens source authority
Occasional informal phrasing and minor typographical errors (e.g., ânumbressâ)
At least one lyric discussion lacks direct quotation for full evidential support
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work moves beyond partial effectiveness to demonstrate clear and coherent understanding of the topic, with accurate use of terminology. Limitations in source type and minor language lapses prevent top-band consistency but overall the criterion is met at the upper halves of the 3â4 band, justifying a score of 5.

strengths: Primary and secondary research is consistently relevant to the research question
Analysis is sustained and well supported, linking imagery and musical devices to the theme of isolation
Arguments are developed into a focused discussion, with critical evaluation in the conclusion
weaknesses: Critical evaluation could further engage counter-arguments for deeper balance
Some analytic claims rely on a limited range of secondary criticism
Minor lapses in developing every interpretive claim to full conclusion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigationâs research quality is strong and reliably pertinent, analysis is insightful and well supported, and the discussion presents a focused, critical argument that synthesizes findings and addresses limitations. Although deeper engagement with academic criticism and more extensive counter-argument would perfect the work, the overall performance meets the top band (10â12). Given the consistent relevance, depth of analysis, and evaluative conclusion, a maximum score of 12 is merited.

strengths: All required sections are present and logically ordered
Table of contents and headings enhance navigability
Works Cited list and in-text citations follow a consistent MLA style
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting in one instance
Spacing and capitalization errors are occasional
Appendices are overly extensive, affecting readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure fully meets Extended Essay requirements, and layout conventions are correctly applied throughout. Minor formatting inconsistencies and an over-long appendix section prevent flawless presentation but do not detract from overall clarity, supporting a full mark of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student outlines a thematic-historical-literary approach and justifies concentrating on three key songs while using others for context.  Primary material (lyrics) is treated as the central source and is supplemented by interviews, reviews and one psychological article.  This constitutes a generally relevant but not especially wide range; important scholarly commentaries on concept albums or progressive-rock narrative are absent.  In-text citation is present but occasionally patchy.  Overall the methodology is mostly complete and shows some informed source selection, meeting the middle band.

Title, word count and table of contents are present; appendices are labelled; in-text citations correspond to a Works Cited list using a recognisable MLA style.  Minor issuesâoccasional spacing errors, inconsistent capitalisation, and an over-long appendices sectionâprevent a perfect score, but overall layout and formatting conventions are correctly applied.

Primary research (close reading of lyrics) is extensive and clearly linked to the question; secondary sourcesâinterviews with Waters, critical essays, psychological studiesâare largely pertinent.  While some sources are popular rather than scholarly, almost all material cited addresses isolation or the album directly, so relevance is high.  The breadth is good though not exhaustive, positioning the strand in the lower end of the 7-9 band.

The student states the album, theme and purpose in the opening sentence and maintains this focus throughout, signposting the exploratory path in the table of contents and section headings.  The guiding ideaâThe Wall as a cautionary tale about isolationâis reiterated in every major section and returned to in the conclusion.  Minor lapses in concision (occasional digressions into background history) do not seriously cloud the overall clarity, so the communication is accurate, purposeful and consistently maintained.

A coherent argumentâthat Pinkâs isolation warns listeners of psychological decayâis followed through the chosen songs and synthesised in the conclusion.  The student acknowledges limitations and suggests areas for further study, showing some evaluative thinking.  Critical self-reflection, counter-argument and nuanced weighing of evidence are limited, preventing entry to the top band, but the discussion is clearly structured and largely persuasive, warranting the upper edge of 4-6.

The student analyses imagery (worms, the wall), tone shifts and musical devices, generally linking them to the theme of isolation.  Assertions are usually supported by lyric quotation or secondary comment, and the essay moves beyond description to interpret implications for audience.  Nonetheless, some sections remain repetitive and occasionally rely on paraphrase rather than deeper textual interrogation, so the analysis is good but not consistently sophisticated, fitting the lower end of 7-9.

The essay contains all required sections (title page with word count, contents, introduction, methodology, argument, conclusion, works cited, appendices).  Headings are used logically, and internal sequencing of ideas is clear.  Therefore the structure is fully appropriate and satisfies EE requirements.

The essay demonstrates solid knowledge of the narrative arc of The Wall and of individual lyrics, weaving them into an argument about isolation.  Historical background on Waters and Barrett is accurately summarised, and secondary material is used to contextualise points.  However, the range of scholarly sources is limited and some references are journalistic websites rather than academic criticism, so application is only partially effective rather than consistently authoritative.

Literary terms such as imagery, metaphor, symbolism, juxtaposition and tone are used correctly and repeatedly.  Musical terminology (riff, solo, rhythm) is applied with reasonable accuracy.  Occasional informal phrasing and minor errors (ânumbressâ) appear, but overall the terminology is appropriate and mostly accurate, meriting the upper-middle band.

The research question, âHow does Pink Floydâs The Wall serve as a cautionary tale through the portrayal of isolation?â, is clearly stated on the title page.  It is sufficiently narrow (one album, one central motif) and all analytical sections link directly back to that question.  Although there are occasional tangents (e.g. brief references to violent-media studies), these are framed as supporting the core inquiry, so the focus remains strong and appropriate for a Category 1 English A investigation.

annotations.comment: The research question is articulated clearly and focused, directly guiding the investigationâs scope.
The word count is accurately stated at 3991 words, meeting the IB requirement and staying under the limit.
The title is clear and informative, immediately orienting the reader to the album, theme, and purpose.
The table of contents is comprehensive and logically ordered, enhancing navigability and focus.
The extensive appendices list may overwhelm readers; consider summarizing or integrating only key lyrics in the main body.
Introduction supplies rich background but spends too much space on band history, which detracts from analytical focus.
Reliance on a journalistic source (Virgin Radio UK) for critical biographical details limits academic rigor; include peer-reviewed scholarship.
The methodology outlines broad approaches but omits clarity on how sources were selected, limiting transparency.
Section header clearly signals analysis of causes and aligns with the research question.
Analysis of âMotherâ quotations is sound, but deeper examination of poetic devices (meter, rhyme) would strengthen the argument.
Discussion of âDonât Leave Me Nowâ lacks direct lyric quotation, weakening evidence-based analysis.
Linking the âballoonsâ imagery to recurring trauma is a strong synthesis of lyrical motifs.
Linking the rumbling noise to cyclical narrative structure is insightful and deepens the thematic reading.
In-text citation for Geddy analysis is inconsistent with Works Cited entry; standardize author/date presentation.
Section âHey Youâ is introduced with clear scope, combining thematic, symbolic, and musical analysis.
Footnote markup appears garbled ("&lt;sup>1"); revise to standard superscript formatting.
Attribution for Waters interview is vague; include speaker, date, and context in-text for clarity.
Instrumental analysis ties musical elements to theme effectively, demonstrating interdisciplinary understanding.
Paragraphs in this section are lengthy; breaking them would improve readability and focus.
The worm metaphor interpretation is insightful and well supported by Watersâ commentary.
Analysis relies heavily on a single secondary source (Urick); broaden to include academic criticism for balance.
Opening analysis of âComfortably Numbâ adeptly establishes emotional tone and situates the songâs thematic importance.
Sentence framing the song as âone of Pink Floydâs most iconicâ is overly informal; refine to maintain academic tone.
Musical terminology (riff, bass, drums) is used precisely, enhancing the depth of lyrical analysis.
The topic sentence for the third-verse discussion lacks clarity; introduce its main analytical focus explicitly.
Analysis of the songâs title reference concludes mid-sentence; ensure each interpretive claim is fully developed.
Integration of Bushman and Andersonâs findings is innovative but needs more direct linkage to specific lyrics.
Presentation of two possible readings of âOutside The Wallâ is clear and invites critical reflection.
The conclusion synthesizes the main arguments lucidly and reiterates the central research question.
Limitations section acknowledges scope but remains descriptive; deepen reflection by evaluating your methodological choices.
Limitations could be expanded to propose specific alternative methods or additional sources for further study.
Works Cited entries are formatted consistently in MLA style, demonstrating strong adherence to presentation conventions.
Citation style varies between DOI, URL, and journal name; standardize formatting across all references.
